patch.value: Derive ALL MCP tool inputSchemas from *_FLAG_CONTRACTS — eliminate hand-declared parallel schema tables (extend the pm_copy pattern)

Class fix behind pm-zx13 (schema/flag drift, closed) and pm-hno5 (pm_copy nested-options drift, closed via PR#545). Today only pm_copy imports COPY_FLAG_CONTRACTS; the other ~27 tools hand-declare schemas that the pm-4os2 golden file can only catch AFTER drift ships. Generating schemas from the single flag-contract source makes CLI/MCP input parity structural. Duplicate check: pm-srns owns OUTPUT envelope grammar; pm-0z7n owns CLI alias sweep; pm-9k90 owns tool TIERING; none owns input-schema derivation. Sequence freely; no blockers.

Every mutation/query MCP tool in src/mcp/tool-definitions.ts derives its flat camelCase inputSchema keys from the corresponding *_FLAG_CONTRACTS table (as pm_copy does from COPY_FLAG_CONTRACTS since PR#545) instead of a hand-declared parallel table; contracts:check snapshot regenerated once and stays byte-stable across a no-op rebuild; a unit spec asserts no tool schema key exists without a backing flag-contract entry (drift becomes structurally impossible, not just snapshot-caught); MCP integration suites stay warning-clean
